title: Rank-Preserving Two-Model Blend With Beta Calibration On OOF Predictions
summary: AUC usually benefits from blending complementary score shapes, and beta calibration is a stronger parametric calibrator than plain Platt scaling when score distributions are skewed.
rationale: AUC usually benefits from blending complementary score shapes, and beta calibration is a stronger parametric calibrator than plain Platt scaling when score distributions are skewed. Even when AUC itself is rank-based, calibrated blending can stabilize public-LB performance when specialist models over- or under-shoot in different regimes.
implementation_hint: Keep the current best tree model, add one complementary specialist (for example ordered CatBoost or the curvature model), generate strict OOF predictions, then fit a simple logistic or beta-calibration meta-layer on the OOF scores only. Compare raw rank average, logistic stack, and beta-calibrated stack on grouped CV.
expected_effect: Likely modest but robust lift from combining different inductive biases while reducing overconfident tails that often hurt leaderboard transfer.
risk: If the specialists are too correlated, the stack adds noise; calibrators must be fit strictly out-of-fold or they will leak.
